The right relationship will add to your life, not drain the fucking life out of you. You should feel more energized, more open, more creative, more like yourself. Not constantly anxious, overthinking, and stuck in survival mode trying to earn basic consistency. Love should have vitality to it. Growth to it. This is How Cardi B Parents Her Children As a Celebrity. A lot of celebrity parents talk about giving their kids “everything,” but Cardi B seems focused on giving her children something completely different. Structure. Expectations. Discipline. Even with millions of dollars and global fame, she still makes sure her kids have tutoring, activities, routines, and responsibilities because she doesn’t want them growing up believing the world automatically owes them something. What makes this interesting is that she openly connects it back to her own childhood and the fear of repeating cycles. She isn’t trying to raise kids who are famous by association. She wants them to become capable people outside of her success. In a culture where wealth often removes struggle entirely, this mindset stands out because it’s less about privilege and more about preparing children for reality before reality prepares them first. #parentingmindset #raisingkids #consciousparenting

Here are a few tips that make a BIG difference when your kid throws food  ➡️ Don’t preload the whole plate: give 2–3 pieces at a time ➡️ Watch for the early signs they’re done (slowing down, playing more than eating- throwing follows!) ➡️ Teach a “finished” cue early (sign)  ➡️ Keep your response boring and predictable every time ➡️ Avoid laughing even once because that’s all it takes to reinforce it ➡️Sit and eat with them when you can bc modeling matters more than you think throwing food is often a communication issue, not a behavior problem. We get to the root of it and the behavior eventually stops! Thanks for sharing your triggering moment 😂 @athomewithnatasha #toddler #infant #babies #motherhood #solids

I’m huge on communication in my classroom and I truly believe even our youngest learners are capable of learning how to express themselves in meaningful ways. Today I modeled exactly how I teach communication skills in my classroom and getting to hear the students’ live reactions and feedback was honestly my favorite part. Moments like this remind me how important it is to give kids the space to use their voice.
